title: A jig assembly
abstract: The present disclosure discloses a jig assembly. The jig assembly comprises a base, and an actuator supported by a support frame extending from the base. The actuator is operable between operating and non-operating positions. The jig assembly, further comprises a holder disposed on the platform defined on the base. The holder is adapted to support a work piece. Further, a plurality of slots are provisioned on the platform of the base to define a path for movement the holder on the base, such that the holder may be positioned in one or more positions on the base. The jig assembly with this configuration provides flexibility to work on multiple locations on a workpiece without the need for changing the holder for holding the workpiece in each location and hence, leads to decrease in cycle time and lead time.
claims: Claims We claim: 1. A jig assembly (200) comprising: a base (100); an actuator (104) supported by a support frame (105) extending from the base (100), wherein the actuator (104) is operable between an operating position and a non- operating position; a holder (101) movably disposed on a platform (100a) defined on the base (100), the holder (101) is adapted to support a workpiece (102); and a plurality of slots (109) provisioned on the platform (100a) of the base (100), wherein, the plurality of slots (109) defines a path for movement of the holder (101) on the base (100) to position the holder (101) in one or more positions on the base (100). 2. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the plurality of slots (109) are formed with progressive plus shaped configuration. 3. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the holder (101) comprises a cavity (111) for supporting the work piece (102) and one or more legs (112) extending from a bottom portion of the cavity (111). 4. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 3, wherein the one or more legs (112) reside inside the plurality of slots (109) such that, movement of holder (101) is restricted within the path defined by the plurality of slots (109). 5. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, comprises a step (110) defined in a plurality of locations in the plurality of slots (109). 6. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, wherein a step (110) defined in each of a plurality of locations is configured to accommodate at least one leg (112) of one or more legs (112) of the holder (101) for locking the holder (101). 7. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 6, wherein each of the plurality of locations corresponds to at least one of the one or more positions of the holder (101) on the base (100). 8. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, wherein each of the one or more positions of the holder (101) on the base (100) corresponds to at least one working region on the workpiece (102). 9. The jig assembly (200) as claimed 1, wherein the actuator (104) supports a tool (103), and the actuator (104) is adapted to advance the tool (103) onto the workpiece (102) in operating position. 10. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the actuator (104) is a spring- loaded actuator. 11. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 1, comprises a handle (106) pivoted to the support frame (105). 12. The jig assembly (200) as claimed in claim 11, wherein the handle (106) is configured to operate the actuator (104) from the non-operating position to the operating position.
